title: Three-way merge engine and conflict resolution policies
purpose: Deliver the three-way merge engine that walks ancestor, parent, and branch trees simultaneously, and the conflict resolution policies (LWW, vector-clock, custom merge function) that determine how true conflicts are resolved. This is the core of haematite's branching value proposition — merge cost proportional to differences, not total data.
task: Implement the three-way merge algorithm that takes two diverged root hashes and their common ancestor. Walk all three trees simultaneously, skipping subtrees where all three hashes match. For subtrees where only one side changed relative to ancestor, apply the changed side (clean merge). For subtrees where both sides changed the same key, route to the conflict resolution policy. Produce the merged result as a single batch tree commit. Implement three policies: LWW (branch wins), vector-clock (detect true conflicts, surface unresolved), and custom merge function (caller-provided). Policy is specified at merge time. Merge operates per-shard. Note: C14/C15 (vector-clock conflict detection and resolution) are covered by defining the ConflictPolicy::VectorClock variant, which returns Err(Unimplemented) in this release. Full implementation is deferred to a future phase.

requirements.title: Three-way tree walk
requirements.spec: WHEN merge(parent_root, branch_root, ancestor_root) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL walk all three trees simultaneously from root to leaves. THE SYSTEM SHALL skip subtrees where all three hashes are identical. THE SYSTEM SHALL descend only into subtrees where at least one hash differs. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT perform a full-tree scan.
acceptance: merge() with identical parent and branch roots returns immediately with no node reads beyond the root
merge() of two branches that each modified 10 keys out of 100,000 reads only the paths to those 20 keys (plus shared traversal nodes)
merge() visits leaf nodes only in subtrees where at least one hash differs across the three trees

files: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:1 — current BRANCH-002 merge stub; this is the primary implementation file for `merge()`, traversal state, merge errors, and tests.
crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs:47-143 — existing structural range-based two-way diff; mirrors the required hash-skipping and child-range alignment patterns.

crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs:311-372 — load/error helpers and child-bound helpers; these are private, so either duplicate the small helpers in `branch/merge.rs` or promote carefully to `pub(crate)`.
crates/haematite/src/tree/node.rs:62-180 — `LeafNode`, `InternalNode`, `Node`, and `Hash` shapes used during simultaneous tree walking.
crates/haematite/src/store/memory.rs:7-13 — `NodeStore` trait read/write contract that merge should accept generically.
context: The tree is content-addressed: `Hash` is `Copy + Eq + Ord`, `Node::hash()` delegates to leaf/internal hashing, and equal hashes are the fundamental subtree equality check.

The existing `tree::diff` function already returns immediately when roots match and recursively skips equal child hashes inside `diff_node_range`; R1 should follow this same structural pattern, not `Cursor::range()` over all keys.

`load_node` in `tree/cursor.rs` is `pub(crate)` and maps store read failures to `TreeError::MissingNode`; `tree::diff` has its own private `DiffError`. Merge will need its own error type or reuse `TreeError` plus policy errors.
Tests should instrument node reads with a custom `NodeStore` wrapper around `MemoryStore`, because `MemoryStore` itself does not count reads.
scout.approach: Define `pub fn merge<S: NodeStore + ?Sized>(store: &mut S, parent_root: Hash, branch_root: Hash, ancestor_root: Hash, policy: &ConflictPolicy) -> Result<Hash, MergeError>` in `branch/merge.rs`. Implement a recursive `merge_node_range(ancestor, parent, branch, lower, upper, out_mutations)` that first returns if all three hashes are equal; then loads only the differing nodes and aligns child ranges exactly like `diff_internal_nodes` does, extended from two to three child sets. For leaf-level ranges, compare the three sorted entry slices by key and append resolved mutations for keys whose final value differs from the parent tree; for internal nodes, recurse only into ranges where at least one of the three child hashes differs. Add fast-path tests for identical roots and sparse edits using a counting store to verify leaf reads occur only under changed paths.
scout.notes: Be careful with the acceptance phrase “no node reads beyond the root”: an implementation can do an even cheaper pre-load equality check (`parent_root == branch_root && branch_root == ancestor_root`) and perform zero reads. If tests are written literally, zero reads is better than reading root nodes; the key is not to descend or scan leaves.

files_changed.note: Implemented the public merge() entrypoint, recursive three-way traversal state, hash-equality skip logic, child-range alignment, and merge errors.
Added read-counting tests for identical-root fast path and sparse structural traversal.

dev.how: merge() now walks ancestor, parent, and branch roots together with NodeHashes/RangeSource state, short-circuiting when parent==branch or branch==ancestor and descending only into aligned child ranges whose hashes require work. Leaf resolution is reached only from differing ranges, and the tests instrument NodeStore reads to prove identical roots do zero reads and sparse changes avoid scanning all leaves.
dev.deviation: merge.rs already existed as a BRANCH-002 stub, so it was modified rather than newly created. Tests were placed in a new merge_tests.rs module via #[path] to keep merge.rs under the repository file-length gate.

checklist.note: merge(store, parent_root, branch_root, ancestor_root, policy) produces a merged root hash from two diverged roots and their common ancestor.
Traversal skips equal-hash subtrees before loading nodes; identical roots return without any node reads.

stories.note: Non-conflicting branch changes are merged back automatically during the structural walk.

acceptance.criterion: merge() with identical parent and branch roots returns immediately with no node reads beyond the root
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:64-65 returns Ok(parent_root) before any load_node call when parent_root == branch_root; test branch::merge::tests::identical_roots_return_without_node_reads at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:181-191 asserts get_count() == 0 and put_count() == 0.

acceptance.criterion: merge() of two branches that each modified 10 keys out of 100,000 reads only the paths to those 20 keys (plus shared traversal nodes)
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:140-147 skips no-op ranges before loading child nodes, and crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:192-235 aligns child ranges by child boundaries instead of scanning leaves. Test branch::merge::tests::sparse_merge_reads_only_changed_paths_not_all_leaves at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:418-447 builds 100,000 keys with 10 parent and 10 branch modifications and verifies the merge does not read all leaves while producing both changed values.

acceptance.criterion: merge() visits leaf nodes only in subtrees where at least one hash differs across the three trees
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:145-147 returns before loading when hashes.merge_is_noop(), crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:149-181 loads and reaches leaf resolution only after that hash-difference gate, and crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:216-222 recurses only on aligned child ranges.

requirements.title: Clean merge detection
requirements.spec: WHILE walking differing subtrees, WHEN only one side (parent or branch) differs from the ancestor for a given key, THE SYSTEM SHALL apply the changed side without invoking conflict resolution. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT treat a one-side-only change as a conflict.
acceptance: A key modified only on the branch side appears in the merged result with the branch value
A key modified only on the parent side appears in the merged result with the parent value
A key deleted only on the branch side is absent in the merged result
No conflict resolution callback is invoked for one-side-only changes

files: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:1 — clean merge classification belongs alongside the three-way leaf/range resolver.
crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs:228-309 — sorted two-way leaf comparison logic; extend this idea to three optional values for ancestor/parent/branch.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:41-57 — `batch_mutate` accepts `(Vec<u8>, Option<Vec<u8>>)` where `None` means deletion; this is the output format clean merges should accumulate.
crates/haematite/src/tree/cursor.rs:46-62 — use `Cursor::get` only in tests/assertions, not during merge traversal, because per-key lookup would destroy the structural merge guarantee.
context: Clean merge means exactly one side changed relative to the ancestor for a key, including insertions, modifications, and deletions represented as `Option<Vec<u8>>`.

The existing leaf entries are sorted and unique by `LeafNode::new`; use a three-way merge over sorted key streams rather than collecting into a `HashMap` that would obscure order and possibly create unsorted mutations.
`batch_mutate` returns the original root for an empty mutation slice, so clean merge code can append only mutations that actually change the parent side.
Conflict callbacks must not be invoked for clean merges; tests should use a custom policy with a call counter/panic to prove this.
scout.approach: Inside the leaf resolver, normalize each side’s key state to `Option<&[u8]>` / cloned `Option<Vec<u8>>` for the next lowest key across ancestor, parent, and branch. Compute `parent_changed = parent_value != ancestor_value` and `branch_changed = branch_value != ancestor_value`; if only branch changed, append `(key, branch_value.clone())` to the batch because the output must differ from parent, and if only parent changed append nothing because the merge base is already the parent tree. If neither changed, append nothing. Keep this resolver separate from policy routing so tests can assert that one-side-only changes bypass conflict policy entirely.
scout.notes: Deletion clean merges are easy to mishandle: branch deletion means `ancestor=Some`, `parent=Some(same as ancestor)`, `branch=None`, and must append `(key, None)` so the final batch deletes from the parent root. Parent-only deletion should append nothing because the parent tree already lacks the key.

files_changed.note: Added three-way leaf key classification for clean parent-only and branch-only changes.
Covered branch-only modification, parent-only modification, branch-only delete, and no custom callback for clean merges.

dev.how: The leaf resolver compares Option<&[u8]> values for ancestor, parent, and branch per sorted key. Parent-only changes append no mutation because the merge base is the parent root; branch-only insert/update/delete appends exactly the branch-side mutation; neither case invokes conflict policy resolution.
dev.deviation: Only test placement deviated from the scout file list: merge behavior tests live in merge_tests.rs to satisfy the file-length gate.

checklist.note: Only-one-side changes relative to the ancestor are applied automatically without invoking ConflictPolicy::resolve.

stories.note: Clean merges promote non-conflicting changes without manual intervention.

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:264-277 detects branch_changed with parent unchanged and pushes the branch value as a mutation; test branch::merge::tests::branch_only_modification_is_applied_without_custom_policy at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:195-213 asserts the merged value is b"branch".

acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:268-270 treats branch unchanged as Ok(()) relative to the parent-root merge base; test branch::merge::tests::parent_only_modification_remains_without_custom_policy at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:216-234 asserts the merged value is b"parent".

acceptance.criterion: A key deleted only on the branch side is absent in the merged result
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:270-275 passes optional_value_to_vec(values.branch), preserving None as a deletion; test branch::merge::tests::branch_only_delete_removes_key at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:237-246 asserts the merged lookup is None.

acceptance.criterion: No conflict resolution callback is invoked for one-side-only changes
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:279-287 calls policy.resolve only in the true (parent_changed, branch_changed) conflict arm; tests branch_only_modification_is_applied_without_custom_policy and parent_only_modification_remains_without_custom_policy at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:197-212 and 218-233 assert CUSTOM_CALLS remains 0.

requirements.title: True conflict detection and routing
requirements.spec: WHEN both the parent and branch have modified the same key relative to the ancestor, THE SYSTEM SHALL detect this as a true conflict and route it to the configured conflict resolution policy. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT silently drop either side's value.
acceptance: A key modified on both sides (different values) is detected as a conflict
A key deleted on one side and modified on the other is detected as a conflict
The conflict resolution policy receives the key, ancestor value, parent value, and branch value
No data is silently discarded

files: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:1 — true conflict detection and routing from the leaf resolver into policy resolution.
crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:1 — conflict input/result types should be defined here so merge does not encode policy-specific logic inline.
crates/haematite/src/tree/diff.rs:253-309 — sorted iterator merge style to identify same-key changes without dropping either side.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:107-146 — mutation application semantics for `Some` replacement and `None` deletion; merge conflict resolution should emit values in this same representation.
context: A true conflict is `parent_changed && branch_changed` for the same key, including modify/modify, delete/modify, modify/delete, and delete/delete if both differ from ancestor but resolve to the same absence.

The conflict policy must receive owned or borrowed access to all four inputs: key, ancestor value, parent value, and branch value. Values need `Option` because deletes and insertions are first-class cases.
No data should be silently discarded: even LWW should be implemented as an explicit policy decision, not as a default branch-wins shortcut in merge.

If both sides changed but converged to the same final value (`parent_value == branch_value`), that can be treated as clean/no-op because no data is discarded; document/test that behavior if implemented.
scout.approach: Add a `Conflict`/`ConflictInput` data shape in `conflict.rs` with fields `key: Vec<u8>`, `ancestor_value: Option<Vec<u8>>`, `parent_value: Option<Vec<u8>>`, and `branch_value: Option<Vec<u8>>` (or borrowed equivalents plus cloning at the boundary). In `merge.rs`, route only `parent_changed && branch_changed && parent_value != branch_value` cases to `policy.resolve(...)`; append the resolved `Option<Vec<u8>>` as a mutation only when it differs from the current parent value. Return a `MergeError` for unresolved conflicts (used by `VectorClock` in this phase) rather than choosing a fallback.
scout.notes: Deletion-vs-modification conflicts require preserving the deleted side as `None`; do not convert missing values to empty `Vec<u8>`, because empty values are valid stored values distinct from deletion.

files_changed.note: Routes true modify/modify and delete/modify conflicts into the selected policy without defaulting to either side.
Defined ConflictInput carrying key plus ancestor, parent, and branch Option<Vec<u8>> values.
Added true-conflict tests for modify/modify and delete/modify routing.

dev.how: A true conflict is detected when both parent and branch differ from the ancestor and their final values differ. The resolver constructs a ConflictInput with owned key/ancestor/parent/branch values, preserving None for deletions, and appends only the explicit policy result as the merge mutation.
dev.deviation: No behavior deviation from the scouted approach; tests were split into merge_tests.rs for repository file-length compliance.

checklist.note: Both-sides-changed conflicts are detected and routed to the configured policy with all values preserved.

stories.note: Callers can choose the conflict policy used for each merge call.

acceptance.criterion: A key modified on both sides (different values) is detected as a conflict
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:264-287 computes parent_changed and branch_changed, then routes differing true conflicts through policy.resolve; test branch::merge::tests::same_roots_can_be_merged_with_different_policies at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:354-373 shows the same modify/modify conflict reaches LWW and Custom policies with different outputs.

acceptance.criterion: A key deleted on one side and modified on the other is detected as a conflict
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:279-287 treats both-sides-changed unequal Option values as a conflict; test branch::merge::tests::delete_modify_conflict_is_routed_to_policy at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:262-280 verifies a parent delete plus branch modify is routed to Custom and resolves to b"k|base|none|branch".

acceptance.criterion: The conflict resolution policy receives the key, ancestor value, parent value, and branch value
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:280-286 constructs ConflictInput with key, ancestor, parent, and branch values; crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:43-48 passes those four borrowed arguments into Custom. The delete/modify test at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:277-280 proves all four values arrive in the callback output.

acceptance.criterion: No data is silently discarded
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:280-287 preserves all three input values in ConflictInput and applies only the explicit policy result; crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:37-49 makes each policy decision explicit, including LWW's branch-value choice and VectorClock's Unimplemented error.

requirements.title: Batch commit of merge result
requirements.spec: WHEN the merge walk is complete, THE SYSTEM SHALL commit all resolved entries to the tree as a single batch — one path-to-root rewrite, not per-entry rewrites. THE SYSTEM SHALL return the new merged root hash.
acceptance: merge() returns a single new root hash
The merged tree contains all entries from both sides with conflicts resolved
The commit produces exactly one tree mutation batch

files: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:1 — final merge should accumulate mutations during traversal and call one commit path at the end.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:41-57 — public `batch_mutate` API; use this once after the traversal, not repeatedly per resolved key.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:282-310 — root finishing behavior shows one path-to-root rewrite from replacement child refs and returns a single root hash.
crates/haematite/src/branch/handle.rs:11-18,186-189 — shard ID and shard iteration conventions if a per-shard helper is added without cross-shard coordination.
crates/haematite/src/branch/fork.rs:28-55 — per-shard branch operations are independent; mirror that boundary if exposing `merge_shards`.
context: `batch_mutate` expects mutations as `&[(Vec<u8>, Option<Vec<u8>>)]` and handles inserts, replacements, and deletes in one traversal/rewrite from the supplied root.

The merge result should be produced by applying accumulated mutations to `parent_root`; parent-only changes therefore do not need mutations, while branch-side clean changes and policy resolutions that differ from parent do.
Existing branch APIs keep per-shard behavior in branch helpers and avoid cross-shard locking or coordination; BRANCH-002 should not add a branch handle merge or global merge coordinator.
`batch_mutate` normalizes only adjacent duplicate keys, so the merge traversal should emit keys in sorted order and avoid duplicate mutations where possible.
scout.approach: Keep `merge()` purely per tree/shard: traverse `ancestor_root`, `parent_root`, and `branch_root` once, collect a sorted `Vec<(Vec<u8>, Option<Vec<u8>>)>` of changes needed relative to the parent tree, and after traversal call `batch_mutate(store, parent_root, mutations.as_slice())` exactly once. If there are no mutations, return `Ok(parent_root)` or the unchanged equivalent without calling `batch_mutate`. If the project wants a multi-shard convenience function, implement it as a thin loop over `(shard_id, parent, branch, ancestor)` that calls the single-shard `merge()` independently per shard and returns a map/vector of roots; do not involve `BranchHandle` WAL buffers or locks.
scout.notes: A tempting optimization is to return `branch_root` when `parent_root == ancestor_root`; that is structurally correct but can bypass the “single batch commit” acceptance. Prefer collecting mutations and using one `batch_mutate` for non-empty merges unless tests/design explicitly allow whole-root reuse.

files_changed.note: Accumulates resolved mutations during traversal and invokes batch_mutate once after the walk.
Compares merge writes against a direct single batch_mutate reference.

dev.how: Merge output is accumulated as a sorted Vec<(Vec<u8>, Option<Vec<u8>>)> relative to the parent root. If the vector is empty, merge returns the parent root; otherwise it calls batch_mutate exactly once, keeping the operation per tree/per shard and avoiding branch handles, WAL buffers, locks, or cross-shard coordination.
dev.deviation: No implementation deviation. The code intentionally does not add a multi-shard coordinator; each shard is represented by one independent tree-root merge call as required by the boundary.

checklist.note: Resolved entries are committed through one final batch_mutate call rather than per-entry rewrites.
merge() is a single-tree/per-shard primitive and introduces no cross-shard coordination.

stories.note: An engine can promote a successful branch by calling merge for each shard root independently.

acceptance.criterion: merge() returns a single new root hash
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:57-63 declares merge(...) -> Result<Hash, MergeError>, and crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:77-80 returns either parent_root or the single Hash returned by batch_mutate.

acceptance.criterion: The merged tree contains all entries from both sides with conflicts resolved
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:264-290 keeps parent-only entries, applies branch-only entries, and resolves true conflicts through the policy before mutation; tests branch_only_modification_is_applied_without_custom_policy, parent_only_modification_remains_without_custom_policy, lww_routes_modify_modify_conflict_to_branch_value, and merge_accumulates_resolutions_into_one_batch cover the resulting tree contents.

acceptance.criterion: The commit produces exactly one tree mutation batch
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:73-80 accumulates all walk mutations and calls batch_mutate exactly once after traversal; test branch::merge::tests::merge_accumulates_resolutions_into_one_batch at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:377-414 compares merge writes to a direct single batch_mutate reference.

requirements.title: Last-write-wins conflict resolution
requirements.spec: WHEN the LWW policy is selected, THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve conflicts by taking the branch value over the parent value. THE SYSTEM SHALL treat LWW as explicitly lossy — the parent's value is discarded.
acceptance: Under LWW, a conflict on key K resolves to the branch value
Under LWW, merge() returns Ok with no unresolved conflicts
The discarded parent value is not present in the merged tree

files: crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:1 — current conflict-policy stub; define `ConflictPolicy`, LWW behavior, and policy errors here.
crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:1 — call the conflict policy only for true conflicts and turn policy outputs into batch mutations.
crates/haematite/src/branch/mod.rs:1-18 — add `pub use conflict::{...}` and possibly `pub use merge::{...}` so callers can select LWW.
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:14-19 — crate root currently re-exports branch APIs manually; update if merge/conflict APIs are intended public.
context: The codebase uses `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]` for simple public error/enums where feasible, plus `Display` and `std::error::Error` implementations for errors.
LWW here is explicitly branch-wins, not timestamp-based: for conflicts, return `branch_value.clone()` even if that is `None` for a branch delete.
Because LWW is lossy by design, implement it only inside `ConflictPolicy::resolve` (or equivalent) so the loss is associated with an explicit selected policy.
`ConflictPolicy::Lww` naming in the brief uses `Lww`, not `LWW`; follow Rust-style enum variant casing.
scout.approach: In `conflict.rs`, define `pub enum ConflictPolicy { Lww, VectorClock, Custom(...) }` and a `resolve` method that accepts key/ancestor/parent/branch values. For `Lww`, return `Ok(branch_value.cloned())` regardless of parent, with tests for branch modification and branch deletion. Merge should use this output and append it as a mutation only when it differs from the parent-side value; under LWW no unresolved-conflict error should be returned.
scout.notes: If `ConflictPolicy` includes a closure/function variant, deriving `Clone`, `PartialEq`, or `Eq` may be impossible; only `Debug` is required by R8. Keep test assertions focused on behavior and `format!("{policy:?}")`, not enum equality if closures are included.

files_changed.note: Added ConflictPolicy::Lww and branch-wins resolution semantics, including branch deletion.
Uses policy output for true conflicts and applies LWW results through the batch mutation pipeline.
Added LWW conflict tests proving branch value wins.

dev.how: ConflictPolicy::Lww returns conflict.branch_value.clone(), making branch-wins loss explicit inside policy resolution rather than hard-coding it in merge. The merge resolver then applies that returned Option<Vec<u8>>, including None for branch-side deletion, only when it differs from the parent value.
dev.deviation: conflict.rs already existed as a BRANCH-002 stub, so it was modified rather than newly created.

checklist.note: LWW resolves conflicts by taking the branch value and discarding the parent value explicitly.

stories.note: LWW is one selectable policy at merge time.

acceptance.criterion: Under LWW, a conflict on key K resolves to the branch value
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:37-40 implements ConflictPolicy::Lww as conflict.branch_value.clone(); test branch::merge::tests::lww_routes_modify_modify_conflict_to_branch_value at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:249-258 asserts key k resolves to b"branch".

acceptance.criterion: Under LWW, merge() returns Ok with no unresolved conflicts
acceptance.evidence: ConflictPolicy::Lww returns Ok(...) at crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:37-40; test branch::merge::tests::lww_routes_modify_modify_conflict_to_branch_value at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:256 uses ? on merge(..., &ConflictPolicy::Lww), so the test would fail on unresolved conflict.

acceptance.criterion: The discarded parent value is not present in the merged tree
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:39 chooses branch_value, and crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:287 applies that result relative to the parent value; test branch::merge::tests::lww_routes_modify_modify_conflict_to_branch_value at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:258 verifies the merged value is b"branch", not b"parent".

requirements.title: Custom merge function
requirements.spec: WHEN a custom merge function is provided, THE SYSTEM SHALL call it with (key, ancestor_value, parent_value, branch_value) for each true conflict. THE SYSTEM SHALL use the function's return value as the resolved value. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT call the function for clean merges.
acceptance: The custom function receives all four arguments (key, ancestor, parent, branch)
The function's return value appears in the merged tree
The function is not called for clean merges (one-side-only changes)
A function that returns None causes the key to be deleted in the merged result

files: crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:1 — define the custom function type and invocation semantics.
crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:1 — ensure custom policy is called only from true-conflict paths, never clean-merge paths.
crates/haematite/src/tree/mutate.rs:41-57 — returned `None` from custom function maps directly to a delete mutation in the final batch.
crates/haematite/Cargo.toml:18-32 — clippy is strict (`unwrap_used`, `expect_used`, `panic` denied in non-test code), so custom policy plumbing must avoid shortcuts.
context: A plain `fn(&[u8], Option<&[u8]>, Option<&[u8]>, Option<&[u8]>) -> Option<Vec<u8>>` variant is easiest to make `Debug`; boxed closures are more flexible but do not implement `Debug` automatically.
The acceptance says `Custom(fn)`, which suggests a function pointer may be sufficient for this phase; if boxed closures are used, wrap them in a newtype with a manual `Debug` implementation.
Custom functions must receive all four arguments, including `None` for delete/missing states, and their `None` return means delete the key from the merged result.
Do not call the custom function for one-side-only changes; this should be covered with a function that increments an atomic counter or returns an obvious sentinel only in conflict tests.
scout.approach: Define a public type alias such as `pub type CustomMergeFn = fn(&[u8], Option<&[u8]>, Option<&[u8]>, Option<&[u8]>) -> Option<Vec<u8>>;` and store it as `ConflictPolicy::Custom(CustomMergeFn)`. In the policy resolver, convert owned conflict values to borrowed slices for the call, then return the function’s `Option<Vec<u8>>` unchanged. Add tests for all four arguments by using a function that asserts key/value combinations through returned encoded output, plus a clean-merge test proving it was not invoked.
scout.notes: If the dev wants closures with captured state for call-count tests, a function-pointer variant will not support captures. Use atomics/static state in tests, or choose `Arc<dyn Fn(...) + Send + Sync>` plus manual `Debug`; the latter is more ergonomic for callers but complicates derivations.

files_changed.note: Added CustomMergeFn and ConflictPolicy::Custom invocation semantics.
Calls custom functions only for true conflicts and maps their Option<Vec<u8>> return directly to merge mutations.
Covered custom argument delivery, clean-merge non-invocation, and custom None deletion.

dev.how: Custom policies use a public function-pointer type accepting key, ancestor_value, parent_value, and branch_value as borrowed optional slices, and returning Option<Vec<u8>>. merge() invokes that function only after true-conflict detection; clean merges bypass it, and None is preserved as a deletion mutation.
dev.deviation: No behavior deviation. The implementation followed the scout’s function-pointer Custom(fn) approach rather than captured closures.

checklist.note: Custom functions receive key plus all three optional values and their returned Option<Vec<u8>> is used as the resolved value.

stories.note: Applications can provide domain-specific conflict resolution logic through ConflictPolicy::Custom.

acceptance.criterion: The custom function receives all four arguments (key, ancestor, parent, branch)
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:3 defines CustomMergeFn as fn(&[u8], Option<&[u8]>, Option<&[u8]>, Option<&[u8]>) -> Option<Vec<u8>>, and crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:43-48 invokes it with key, ancestor_value, parent_value, and branch_value.

acceptance.criterion: The function's return value appears in the merged tree
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:286-287 uses the policy's returned Option<Vec<u8>> as the resolved mutation; test branch::merge::tests::same_roots_can_be_merged_with_different_policies at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:361-373 asserts the custom return b"k|base|parent|branch" appears in the merged tree.

acceptance.criterion: The function is not called for clean merges (one-side-only changes)
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:268-287 calls policy.resolve only for true conflicts; tests at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:197-212 and 218-233 assert CUSTOM_CALLS stays 0 for branch-only and parent-only clean merges.

acceptance.criterion: A function that returns None causes the key to be deleted in the merged result
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:293-301 pushes a mutation when resolved_value differs from parent_value, including None; test branch::merge::tests::custom_policy_returning_none_deletes_key at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:284-307 asserts delete-me is absent after the custom policy returns None.

requirements.title: Policy selection at merge time
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL accept the conflict resolution policy as a parameter to merge(), not as a property of the branch. THE SYSTEM SHALL support LWW, VectorClock, and Custom(fn) as policy variants.
acceptance: merge() takes a ConflictPolicy parameter
ConflictPolicy is an enum with Lww, VectorClock, and Custom variants
The same branch can be merged with different policies in different calls
ConflictPolicy implements Debug

files: crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:1 — define `ConflictPolicy::{Lww, VectorClock, Custom}` and policy error/result types.
crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:1 — `merge()` signature must take a policy parameter at call time and treat `VectorClock` as unimplemented in this phase.
crates/haematite/src/branch/mod.rs:11-18 — add re-exports for `ConflictPolicy`, `MergeError`, `merge`, and any helper result types.
crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:14-31 — update crate-level exports if public API tests import from `haematite::ConflictPolicy`/`haematite::merge`.
docs/design/branching/briefs/BRANCH-002.json:180-214 — confirms `VectorClock` variant is required but full vector-clock resolution is explicitly out of scope and should return `Err(Unimplemented)`.
context: Policy selection must be a parameter to `merge()`, not stored on `BranchHandle`; existing `BranchHandle` only tracks roots and WAL buffers, and BRANCH-002 boundaries say not to implement branch handle/fork changes.
`ConflictPolicy` must implement `Debug`; with a custom function pointer this can be derived, but with trait objects a manual `Debug` implementation is required.
`VectorClock` should exist as a variant and route conflicts to an unimplemented error; do not attempt to add per-key version vectors or causal metadata in this branch.
The same branch/root triplet can be merged multiple times with different policies because all policy state is call-local and no merge history/log should be introduced.
scout.approach: Expose `merge(store, parent_root, branch_root, ancestor_root, policy)` (by value or `&ConflictPolicy`) and define `MergeError` with variants for tree failures, unresolved conflicts, and `Unimplemented`/`VectorClockUnimplemented`. Keep `ConflictPolicy` in `conflict.rs` and make `merge.rs` import it; re-export both through `branch/mod.rs` and optionally `lib.rs`. For `VectorClock`, immediately return `Err(MergeError::Unimplemented { feature: "vector-clock conflict resolution" })` when a true conflict reaches policy resolution; clean merges under `VectorClock` could still succeed if no conflict occurs, but tests may also accept failing when selected—prefer conflict-only failure to preserve clean-merge usefulness.
scout.notes: Do not add vector-clock data structures to tree entries or branch state in this phase. Also avoid naming the function `sync::merge` or touching `crates/haematite/src/sync/merge.rs`; BRANCH-002 belongs under `branch::merge`.

files_changed.note: Defined Debug ConflictPolicy variants Lww, VectorClock, and Custom(CustomMergeFn), plus policy error handling.
merge() accepts the conflict policy at call time and surfaces vector-clock true conflicts as Unimplemented.
Re-exported merge and conflict policy APIs from the branch module.
Re-exported merge and conflict policy APIs from the crate root.
Covered policy selection, reusing the same roots with different policies, and VectorClock deferred behavior.

dev.how: Conflict policy is a merge() parameter rather than branch state, so the same root triplet can be merged with Lww or Custom in separate calls. VectorClock is exposed as a Debug enum variant and returns an Unimplemented error when a true conflict reaches policy resolution, while clean merges still succeed without vector-clock resolution.
dev.deviation: The literal per-key vector-clock data model was not implemented because the brief boundary explicitly says not to implement vector-clock conflict resolution in BRANCH-002 and to return Err(Unimplemented) instead.

checklist.note: Policy is supplied to merge() at call time and is not stored on BranchHandle or fork state.
For this phase, VectorClock support is the required selectable variant and deferred error path; per-key version-vector tracking/resolution was intentionally not added per the explicit boundary.
True conflicts under VectorClock are surfaced to the caller as MergeError::Unimplemented rather than being silently dropped.

stories.note: The same branch/root triplet can be merged with different policies in different calls.
Existing public diff APIs remain exported; BRANCH-002 policy selection did not regress snapshot diffing support.

acceptance.criterion: merge() takes a ConflictPolicy parameter
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/merge.rs:57-63 includes policy: &ConflictPolicy in the merge signature; the API is re-exported in crates/haematite/src/branch/mod.rs:12-15 and crates/haematite/src/lib.rs:14-19.

acceptance.criterion: ConflictPolicy is an enum with Lww, VectorClock, and Custom variants
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:29-33 defines pub enum ConflictPolicy { Lww, VectorClock, Custom(CustomMergeFn) }. VectorClock returns Err(Unimplemented) at crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:40-42, matching this brief's explicit vector-clock deferral boundary.

acceptance.criterion: The same branch can be merged with different policies in different calls
acceptance.evidence: test branch::merge::tests::same_roots_can_be_merged_with_different_policies at crates/haematite/src/branch/merge_tests.rs:354-373 merges the same ancestor/parent/branch roots with Lww and Custom and verifies distinct policy-specific results.

acceptance.criterion: ConflictPolicy implements Debug
acceptance.evidence: crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:29 derives Debug for ConflictPolicy; test branch::conflict::tests::conflict_policy_is_debuggable at crates/haematite/src/branch/conflict.rs:165-170 formats Lww, VectorClock, and Custom with {:?}.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement fork or branch handle — those are BRANCH-001
SHALL NOT implement snapshot registry or time-travel — those are BRANCH-003
SHALL NOT implement snapshot pruning — that is BRANCH-004
SHALL NOT implement cross-shard merge coordination — each shard merges independently
SHALL NOT implement merge history tracking or merge commit log
SHALL NOT implement vector-clock conflict resolution — deferred to future phase. ConflictPolicy::VectorClock returns Err(Unimplemented).
